content.claim: Hungary's socioeconomic achievement gap is among the largest in the OECD: socioeconomic background explains 23.1% of student performance variance, compared to the OECD average of 14.8%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: This measures correlation between background and achievement, not the causal contribution of early tracking to this gap.
The gap existed before Hungary's current tracking system and may reflect historical, labour-market, or family-investment factors independent of school selection age.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
